subject: Databases
title: Temporal Tables
description: A fast breakdown of Temporal Tables for builders.

hook.text: SQL:2011 introduced system-versioned temporal tables. The database tracks row history automatically.

buildup.text: Every row has valid_from and valid_to timestamps. Updates create a new current row and archive the old one.

discovery.text: Query the past: SELECT * FROM products FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF '2024-01-15'. The database returns that day's data.

twist.text: MariaDB and SQL Server support temporal tables natively. Postgres doesn't yet, but extensions exist.

climax.text: Temporal tables simplify auditing, compliance, and debugging. History is automatic, not hand-coded.

punchline.text: Temporal tables: automatic history. Query any point in time with standard SQL.

quiz.question: What does the FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF clause do?
options: It queries the table as it existed at a specific point in time, using the database's version history
It sets the system clock to a different time
It creates a backup of the table at the specified time
